name: NIST CSF 2.0 MCP Server
what_it_does: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 MCP server enabling AI agents to query and apply the NIST CSF 2.0 framework — retrieving framework functions, categories, and subcategories, mapping controls to CSF requirements, supporting security assessment workflows, and integrating NIST's comprehensive cybersecurity guidance into agent-driven security risk management and compliance workflows.
use_cases: Mapping security controls to NIST CSF 2.0 categories from compliance agents
Assessing organizational security posture against CSF from audit agents
Generating CSF-aligned security roadmaps from risk management agents
Retrieving specific CSF subcategory guidance from security advisory agents
Building CSF-based security questionnaires from assessment agents
Training and educating on NIST CSF from security awareness agents
not_for: Teams using ISO 27001, SOC 2, or other frameworks exclusively (different frameworks)
Technical vulnerability scanning (CSF is governance/framework, not technical scanning)
Automated compliance checking against real infrastructure
best_when: An agent needs to work with NIST CSF 2.0 guidance — for security program development, compliance mapping, risk assessments, or CSF-based security advisory work.
avoid_when: You need technical vulnerability data, real-time threat intelligence, or compliance checking against ISO 27001 or GDPR rather than NIST CSF.

auth.notes: No authentication required — the NIST CSF 2.0 content is static, open framework data. No external API calls needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — NIST CSF is public domain and the MCP is open source
pricing.notes: NIST CSF 2.0 is a public domain U.S. government document. MCP server bundles the framework data locally. No external costs.

performance.rate_limits: Local static data — no external calls, no rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: CSF data is static — updates when NIST releases new framework versions require MCP updates
CSF subcategory IDs use specific notation (GV.OC-01) — agents must know the format
Framework is governance-level — agents cannot directly map controls without human context
Community MCP — framework accuracy should be verified against official NIST publications
CSF 2.0 added Govern function — ensure MCP includes latest CSF 2.0 content, not CSF 1.1
Sector-specific profiles and informative references not included in base CSF
agent_readiness.security_notes: Static local data. No network access. No secrets needed. Community MCP. Verify framework data accuracy against official NIST publications for compliance-critical use.

security_score_components.security_notes: Static local data. No network. No secrets. Community MCP. Verify against official NIST docs for compliance use.
